title: Rare Axolotl Discovered in Welsh River by Young Girl
summary: A 10-year-old girl, Evie Hill, made a remarkable discovery while playing in the River Ogmore in Bridgend, Wales: a nine-inch Mexican axolotl, an endangered amphibian. Evie found the axolotl, later named Dippy, under a discarded mat in the shallows near the 'Dipping Bridge' at Merthyr Mawr. The axolotl had visible injuries to its tail and stomach. Experts confirm this is the first documented wild axolotl in the UK. Axolotls are critically endangered, with only 50 to 1,000 individuals remaining in the wild globally. Their popularity as pets has increased due to appearances in video games like Minecraft and Roblox. Evie's mother, Melanie, was initially shocked but noted her daughter's keen interest in nature. The family cut their campervan holiday short to return to Leicester and care for Dippy. Chris Newman, director of the National Centre for Reptile Welfare, praised Evie for potentially saving the axolotl's life. The discovery highlights the risks of exotic pets being released into the wild, as axolotls are not native to the UK and may struggle to survive.

background_read: Axolotls are native to Mexico and are critically endangered due to habitat loss and pollution. They are often kept as pets and have become popular in pop culture. Finding one in the UK wild is unusual and likely the result of a pet being released.
Article_Structure: Main Points: A 10-year-old girl found a rare Mexican axolotl in a Welsh river, the first documented wild axolotl in the UK. The axolotl was injured and taken home by the family. Purpose: To report a unique wildlife discovery and raise awareness about endangered species and exotic pet ownership. Evidence Evaluation: The article includes quotes from the mother and an expert, providing firsthand and professional perspectives. Author Credibility: BBC News is a reputable news source. Methodology: The article is based on interviews and expert comments.

perspectives.description: The discovery shows how exotic pets can end up in the wild, potentially harming local ecosystems.
Evie's love for nature led to an important scientific finding.
